Title: Adaptive protein evolution through length variation of short tandem repeats in Arabidopsis

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; CS77239; transient assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TCP family transcription factor; contains variable polyglutamine (poly-Q) tracts in intrinsically disordered/protein-protein interaction-prone regions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Different TCP14 poly-Q tract lengths produce significantly different ELIP1 promoter activities; the second poly-Q tract has stronger influence, and both tracts together modulate activation strength.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photoprotective/light-stress transcriptional response associated with chlorophyll biosynthesis and chloroplast biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ELIP1 promoter; activity is repressed by OR interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of ELIP1; likely modulated by poly-Q-dependent protein interaction properties
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physical interaction with OR in the nucleus is reported; OR represses TCP14 transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Illumination/high light; environmental gradients including global horizontal irradiation, temperature, and soil silt content associated with poly-Q tract variation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light stress; illumination; 10 mM estradiol induction in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; ELIP1 promoter luciferase assay; GFP localization; comparison of natural and synthetic poly-Q length variants
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 770 Arabidopsis thaliana accessions from the 1001 Genomes resource; environmental association analyses; no accession number reported in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Poly-Q tract editing in TCP14 could tune light-stress transcriptional responses and chloroplast biogenesis, but no nutrient-metabolite engineering application was directly demonstrated.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP14 is the experimentally tested core gene in the Results section. 2. Natural allelic variation in two STR-encoded poly-Q tracts changes TCP14-mediated activation of the ELIP1 promoter. 3. The last poly-Q tract has a stronger effect than the first, and joint tract variation significantly changes transactivation output. 4. TCP14 poly-Q variants correlate with environmental variables including irradiation, temperature, and edaphic factors.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper is not centered on nutrient metabolism. ELIP1, chlorophyll biosynthesis, and OR are discussed as downstream/context genes, but the direct experimentally tested focal regulator is TCP14. No terminal nutritional metabolite was quantified.
